title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Runtime Contract: Denial Disclosure Audit
summary: Authenticated users can enumerate another tenant’s records, mass-assign privileged fields, call admin actions through hidden routes, and infer denied objects from response differences. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo denial disclosure audit increment in deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 consistently, limit side channels, record privacy-safe decision evidence, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
Enforce function-level policy at a shared application boundary while retaining resource-specific checks and testing every route alias.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Denial Disclosure Audit · Theory Denial Disclosure Audit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8294 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 consistently, limit side channels, record privacy-safe decision evidence, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
